div1.entryFree: Wight, person, being (fem. as well as masc.): beshrew the witch (viz night)! with venomous --s she stays as tediously as hell, Troil. IV, 2, 12. Used by Pistol: O base Hungarian w. Wiv. I, 3, 23. I ken the w. Wiv. I, 3, 23 O braggart vile and damned furious w. H5 II, 1, 64. In rhyming: Sonn. 106, 2. LLL I, 1, 178. In the style of popular poetry: she was a w., if ever such w. were, Oth. II, 1, 159. he was a w. of high renown, II, 3, 96. so for her many a w. did die, II, 3, 96 (Gower's speech).
